metadata.title: ContextPacket — minimal evidence set for constitutional rule evaluation

description: Constitution-aligned context packet (src/shared/infrastructure/context/models.py). A ContextPacket is the minimal evidence set required to evaluate applicable rules at a declared CORE phase. Passed to LLM invocations as the structured context envelope. The eight dict[str, Any] slots (header, layer_constraints, constitution, policy, constraints, evidence, runtime, provenance) are ungoverned beyond dict[str, Any] — slot-level contracts are Wave 2 scope. ContextPackage = ContextPacket is a module-level alias for backward compatibility during migration; it is a variable assignment, not a class, and is not governed here. Authority: ADR-056 Wave 1 Session 4.

request.description: The ContextBuildRequest that initiated this packet assembly. Governed by the ContextBuildRequest contract.

header.description: Packet header metadata. Shape ungoverned at this level. Default empty dict.

layer_constraints.description: Layer-specific constitutional constraints applicable to the request. Default empty dict.

constitution.description: Constitutional rules relevant to the request phase and target. Default empty dict.

policy.description: Policy rules relevant to the request. Default empty dict.

constraints.description: Additional constraints derived from the request context. Default empty dict.

evidence.description: Evidence gathered to support rule evaluation — symbols, vectors, history. Default empty dict.

runtime.description: Runtime state snapshot relevant to the request. Default empty dict.

provenance.description: Traceability metadata: who built this packet, when, from what sources. Default empty dict.
